title: Plocama reboudiana
intro: Plocama reboudiana, also known as Reboud's Plocama, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the western Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Plocama reboudiana include limonoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Plocama reboudiana is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its potential skin benefits.
taxonomy: Plocama reboudiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a type of evergreen shrub that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is also known as the "tree broom" due to its unique growth habit. The classification of Plocama reboudiana is as follows: it belongs to the genus Plocama, and then to the subfamily Rutoideae within the family Rutaceae. It is also classified as a member of the order Sapindales.
